requirement.title: Block on Design Contradictions
requirement.description: Refiner MUST block when the design doc contains contradictory requirements, per Rule R3 (Block on Contradictions).

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Given a design doc where REQ-A requires "sessions expire after 15 minutes" and REQ-B requires "sessions never expire", refiner MUST block and produce a refiner-report.md. REQ-F008
When blocked, refiner MUST NOT produce or update any spec.xml or details/ files. REQ-F008

requirement.title: Rejection Report
requirement.description: When refiner blocks, the rejection report MUST be stored alongside the rejected design doc at docs/plans/<spec-id>/<date>/refiner-report.md.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: The refiner-report.md MUST be at the same directory level as the rejected design.md (e.g., docs/plans/my-feature/2026-04-16/refiner-report.md). REQ-F009
The report MUST list each contradiction, missing acceptance criterion, and broken dependency with specific requirement IDs. REQ-F009
The report MUST include a Recommendations section with a specific suggestion for resolving each listed issue. REQ-F009

requirement.title: Spec Identity Header
requirement.description: Every spec.xml MUST contain an identity header with spec_id, spec_version, status, source (design path + iteration date), scope, and supersedes fields.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: A newly created spec.xml MUST have spec_version: 1, status: active, and supersedes empty or absent. REQ-F010
An updated spec.xml MUST have spec_version: base_version + 1, and supersedes set to "<spec-id>:v<previous-version>". REQ-F010
The source element MUST contain design_path pointing to the exact design doc file and design_iteration matching the YYYY-MM-DD iteration date. REQ-F010
The scope element MUST list included features with IDs and excluded items with reasons. REQ-F010

requirement.title: Per-Spec Directory Isolation
requirement.description: Multiple specs MUST coexist in separate specs/<spec-id>/ folders. Each folder is a self-contained project unit.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Given spec-ids "auth" and "payments", their specs MUST reside at specs/auth/spec.xml and specs/payments/spec.xml respectively, with no cross-folder references. REQ-F011
Each spec folder MUST contain its own details/ subdirectory. Detail files MUST NOT reference requirements from other spec folders. REQ-F011

requirement.title: Initial Mode — Formalize From Scratch
requirement.description: When REFINER_INPUT type is "initial", refiner MUST formalize the entire design doc into a new spec.xml v1 and details/ files.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Given a design doc with type: initial and 10 functional requirements, refiner MUST produce a spec.xml with spec_version: 1 and detail files covering all 10 requirements. REQ-F012
Every requirement in the REFINER_INPUT MUST appear as a <requirement> element in one of the detail files, with at least one acceptance criterion. REQ-F012
Every acceptance criterion MUST have a <trace> element referencing the source requirement ID from the design doc. REQ-F012

requirement.title: Iteration Mode — Delta Application
requirement.description: When REFINER_INPUT type is "iteration" and a Delta section with content exists, refiner MUST apply ADDED/MODIFIED/REMOVED operations to the existing spec.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: ADDED items MUST result in new <requirement> elements added to the appropriate detail file, with new acceptance criteria and traces. REQ-F013
MODIFIED items MUST update the matching <requirement> element in place. The requirement's description, title, and acceptance criteria MUST reflect the modification. REQ-F013
REMOVED items MUST delete the matching <requirement> element from its detail file. If a detail file becomes empty, the file MUST be deleted and its reference removed from spec.xml. REQ-F013
Requirements NOT mentioned in the Delta MUST remain unchanged in the output spec. REQ-F013

requirement.title: Replace Mode — Full Rewrite
requirement.description: When REFINER_INPUT type is "iteration" and the Delta section is absent (replaced by full requirements), refiner MUST formalize from scratch as v(N+1), superseding the previous version.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Given type: iteration, base_version: 2, and no "### Delta" heading in the REFINER_INPUT, refiner MUST produce spec.xml v3 from scratch. REQ-F014
The supersedes field MUST be set to "<spec-id>:v<base_version>". REQ-F014
All detail files from the previous version MUST be replaced (not merged) with detail files derived from the new full requirements. REQ-F014

requirement.title: Merged Spec Validation
requirement.description: After applying delta or producing a new spec, refiner MUST validate that the resulting spec has no internal contradictions before writing output.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Given a delta that ADDs a requirement contradicting an existing unchanged requirement, refiner MUST block and produce a refiner-report.md. REQ-F015
Given a delta that MODIFIEs a requirement into contradiction with another requirement, refiner MUST block. REQ-F015
Given a delta that REMOVEs a requirement depended on by another requirement still in the spec, refiner MUST block with a "dependency broken" issue. REQ-F015

requirement.title: Empty Delta Detection (Strategy 3)
requirement.description: When REFINER_INPUT type is "iteration" and a "### Delta" heading exists but contains no ADDED/MODIFIED/REMOVED items, refiner MUST block. An empty delta is an ambiguous signal — neither a clear no-op nor a clear replace.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Given a design doc with type: iteration, base_version: 2, and a "### Delta" heading followed by no items (empty or whitespace-only), refiner MUST block. REQ-F008, REQ-F014

The refiner-report.md MUST state the issue as "ambiguous delta: ### Delta heading present but no ADDED/MODIFIED/REMOVED items" and recommend either removing the heading (for replace) or adding delta items (for iteration). REQ-F009
